creator: okor@zone.salut.ru
text:
rwatson@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: panic: sorele. FreeBSD 6.1 RELEASE i386
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: rwatson
> State-Changed-When: Fri Mar 7 18:45:29 UTC 2008
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Could you confirm whether this still happens with FreeBSD 6.2 and/or
> FreeBSD 6.3?  Many bugs with these symptoms were fixed between 6.1 and
> 6.2, and further races were fixed for 6.3.  Thanks!
> 
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=100974
> 

Hello Robert,

I'm very glad to see your email, since this error doesn't allow us to do 
'debug.mpsafenet=1' on our http/ftp servers.

This panic still here (in 6.2 and 6.3) and as far as I can investigate, error 
is connected to socket passing, where one process can pass open file (socket) 
descriptor to another using sendto/recvfrom via SCM_RIGHTS, see recv(2) for 
details.

The software that causes this panic is FTP server which runs as two distinct 
processes: the master process and the worker process. Master process runs with 
credentials of privileged user, supervises worker process and provide  worker 
process with necessary privileged operations (socket binding). The worker 
process does all ftp server work and completely drops privileges, worker relies 
on master process for any privileged operations.

So worker process sends requests to bind socket (to privileged port 20) to 
master process, master process creates socket, binds it to passed address, 
initiates connect and than passes socket back to worker. Under real workload 
this causes panics at random time (from some hours to some days).

The exact sequence of operations is:
worker: sendmsg() #sends request with `bind' and `connect to' addresses.
master: recvmsg() #receives request.
	socket()
	setsockopt(SO_REUSEADDR)
	bind()
	connect()
	sendto() #sends socket back to worker.
	close()	#closes passed socket in master process.
worker: recvmsg() # with socket or errno if socket/bind/connect fails.

All operations are asynchronous, such a way worker can send a bunch of requests 
and then receive them as a bunch too.

Thanks for sincere efforts to make FreeBSD a productive OS.


===

Below is a PART of code that leads to this panics:
